programmes.show_description: MG/GN Racecars/Honda Prelude: Dave collects a barely used 1984 Honda Prelude from one of his all-time heroes - motorcycle legend, Ron Haslam.
Nash Metropolitan: Derek collects five beautiful veteran motorbikes from an enthusiast who's still happily riding in his 90s.
Ford Capri Consort: A rare 1964 Ford Capri Consul coupe comes up for auction. Paul takes a 1957 Leyland Comet truck for a spin, and Dave admits that high on his wish-list is a Citroen 2CV.

programmes.show_name: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programmes.show_description: The American Civil War: Lucy Worsley shows how the history of the American Civil War has been retold to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division.
Shopping from Home.
Ballymena to Carrickfergus: Michael Portillo visits a museum in Belfast Lough telling the story of Ireland's railway heritage and learns of the important role of Carrickfergus.
Bangor to Belfast: Michael Portillo makes his way from Bangor to Belfast, along the way enjoying live music on a boat and visiting the parliament building at Stormont.
Carlingford to Dublin: Michael Portillo makes his way from the vast beaches of the Carlingford Peninsula to the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin.
Dun Laoghaire to Rosslare: Michael Portillo travels from the capital of the Republic of Ireland, Dublin, on the DART suburban railway to the harbour town of Dun Laoghaire.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: How to Build a London Cab: With factory access, see how a 21st century electric London Taxi is made in 28 hours.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: How to Build the Fastest Car: A team of plucky Brits attempt to prove that they've built the world's fastest car.

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Mini: An access-all-areas look at how the classic Mini is being manufactured for the electric revolution. What is needed to create the components of a Mini Electric?

programmes.show_name: How To Build A Motor Car
programmes.show_description: Jaguar F-Type: Jaguar gives us an access-all-areas look at how the F-TYPE is built from scratch, from its sleek chassis to an engine that can reach top speeds of 177mph.
The Gestapo Ruins a Picnic: 1980s sitcom that finds farcical fun in the French Resistance. Von Klinkerhoffen wants to use the local newspaper for Nazi propaganda and asks Rene to run it.
The Spirit of Nouvion: Another merry trip to wartime France. Michelle enlists Rene to try to retrieve the Fallen Madonna locked in Herr Flick's safe. What could possibly go wrong!?
Leg it to Spain: Farcical sitcom with Gorden Kaye as amorous cafe owner Rene Artois. Those bumbling British airmen are trapped in wine barrels in the cellars of the chateau.
Weybridge to Stonebridge Park: Robbie moors by Hampton Court Palace, but disaster strikes when a tree branch smashes a window. His dad joins to help through Hanwell locks.
Harlesden to Stratford: Robbie returns to where his boating life began, exploring London's canals on Naughty Lass. He travels through Little Venice and moors in Paddington Basin.
Cornish newcomer Dom Chinea tries to fix a leaky sea pool near Mousehole. He buys a battered old Land Rover and starts a new project crushing waste concrete to fill potholes on his lane.
The second leg of Simon Reeve's journey around the Caribbean Sea takes him from the beautiful island of Barbados to St Vincent and the Grenadines, Venezuela and Colombia.
Simon's last leg begins on the remote Caribbean coast of Nicaragua before he encounters extreme violence on the mainland of Honduras and finishes his adventure on Jamaica.
Daimler Sovereign Coupe: Irv and the team update a 1977 Daimler Sovereign Coupe giving it a slick, modern makeover. However, a disaster in the paint booth puts the project in peril.

Tesla Model X/Showman's Caravan: Paul, Mick and Charlie are in Hull for one of their biggest ever collections. Dave takes a test drive in Tesla Model X and Derek is bowled over by a classic vintage mo
Rascal / Triumph TR6: The team head for restoration in their Bedford Rascal Campervan. They also purchase a true British classic - a TR6 - which is almost too good to touch!
Dauphine / Omega: A super low mileage Renault Dauphine proves irresistible to the Resto team... But its 1959 mechanics ensure some hefty bills.
Ford Thames 400E/Vauxhall Astra: Derek collects a Ford Thames 400E that once belonged to the pop band Edison Lighthouse. Plus, Dave heads to York to collect an Astra GTE convertible.
Ford Zodiac Estate/Toledo: Derek picks up a rare Ford Zodiac Farnham Estate, while Dave heads for an isolated tent in the Midlands to collect a Triumph Toledo.
Prior Engagements: French farce and inspired innuendo in the WWII sitcom. In an effort to rustle up useful propaganda, Von Klinkerhoffen orders Edith to marry Captain Bertorelli.
Soup and Sausage: Slightly saucy wartime farce. The British airmen are stuck down the drain in the village square. Rene plans to get them out using an old ice cream van. Simple!
Rene of the Gypsies: More laughs at the expense of every power player in the EU! Michelle plans to release the airmen from the drain in the square under cover of a gypsy fair.
Shopping from Home.
Weymouth to Lulworth: Michael Portillo travels across Dorset, beginning in Portland Harbour, the location for the 2012 Olympic sailing events.
Kimmeridge to Brownsea Island: Michael Portillo visits Dorset's Jurassic Coast. He heads through Swanage, Poole and Sandbanks, before ending his journey on Brownsea Island.
Southampton to Isle of Wight: Michael Portillo heads from Southampton to the Isle of Wight, where he rides the island's railway, made up of recycled London Underground trains.
Lee-on-the-Solent to Bosham: Michael Portillo explores Lee on the Solent in Hampshire, the maritime city of Portsmouth, and Chichester's vast, natural harbour.

Shardlow to Stoke-on-Trent: Robbie Cumming journeys from Shardlow to Etruria Junction, Stoke on Trent, exploring the Trent and Mersey Canal.
Froghall to Bugsworth Basin: Robbie Cumming explores the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater.
Marple to Huddersfield: Robbie Cumming continues across the Pennines from Marple to Huddersfield, where his journey is hampered by a fallen tree and a leaking lock pound.
Sowerby Bridge to Manchester: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester, but disaster strikes when he runs aground.

East: Actor Martin Clunes explores Lord Howe Island, Norfolk Island and Restoration Island.
Martin Clunes travels to the Tiwi Islands, swims with a whale shark, visits the Houtman Abrolhos, and samples life on Rottnest Island.

programmes.show_name: This World: Burma With Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Simon Reeve ventures into Burma's highlands, where he attends a fire balloon festival, visits an elephant sanctuary, and goes in search of a rebel army.

programmes.show_name: This World: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programmes.show_description: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to one of the most spectacular countries in the world, Colombia, to explore the country's precarious future.

Paul Merton and wife Suki Webster drive off for a holiday-at-home in Cornwall with spectacular sea views, beautiful beaches and a birthday trip to a vineyard.
